title: Fuzzer: DuckDB Internal Error with the COALESCE function
description:
### What happens?

DuckDB throws an internal error with the COALESCE function.

### To Reproduce

PoC:
```sql
SELECT COALESCE(NULL + NULL);
```

Output:
```
INTERNAL Error: Comparison on NULL values
```

### OS:

Ubuntu 20.04

### DuckDB Version:

v1.0.0 and v1.0.1-dev3611

### DuckDB Client:

Cli

### Full Name:

Jingzhou Fu

### Affiliation:

Wingtecher Lab of Tsinghua University and ShuiMuYuLin Ltd.

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a source build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Not applicable - the reproduction does not require a data set

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, value1 INTEGER, value2 INTEGER);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value1, value2) VALUES (1, NULL, NULL);
SELECT COALESCE(value1, value2) FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT COALESCE(0 + value1, 0 + value2) FROM test_table;
SELECT COALESCE(NULL, NULL, NULL) AS result;
key_bug_features: COALESCE function with NULL values causes internal error
